source_metadata.title_raw: Angie Palmerin, Esq. - A-19-033 - March 13, 2019 - West Sacramento

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 13, 2019

Angie Palmerin, Esq.
3706 Abaco Road
West Sacramento, CA 95691

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-033

Dear Ms. Palmerin:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of the Political
Reform Act ("Act").¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the gift provisions of the
Act and not under other prohibitions, such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not
the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

QUESTION

As a public official subject to the Act's gift provisions, is Western Association of Food
Chains' ("WAFC") waiver of your husband's registration fee considered a gift to you?

CONCLUSION

No. Under the facts presented, you will not receive a gift as defined by Section 82028(a).

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You are a staff attorney with the Department of Forestry and Fire Protection ("Cal Fire")
holding a position designated in the agency's conflict-of-interest code. Your duties consist mainly
of providing legal advice as it relates to Cal Fire's recovery program, which seeks to recover
suppression costs incurred during fire suppression activities.

Your husband has been in-house counsel for Raley's Supermarkets since January 2014.
Raley's is a privately-held supermarket chain that operates stores throughout Northern California

___________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-033
Page No. 2

and Nevada. Although Cal Fire may purchase goods from Riley's during fire incidents, you have
never been involved in making decisions or providing advice regarding grocery retailers.

Your husband is attending a semester long Food Industry Management ("FIM") program at
the University of Southern California ("USC"). Your husband's FIM class is invited to speak at
and/or attend the Western Association of Food Chains ("WAFC") annual convention. The WAFC is
an organization focused on providing education opportunities for individuals working in the food
industry. According to its website, the WAFC is a non-profit organization under Internal Revenue
Code Section 501(c)(3). The WAFC helped establish USC's FIM program. As part of the
relationship between USC and WAFC, the WAFC waives the registration fee associated with the
convention for students of the FIM program, but the students' employers, or the students
themselves, are responsible for paying for hotel accommodations. The WAFC annual conference
will be in Palm Desert and take place from May 4-8, 2019. Included with the convention are a
number of meals, presentations, and educational material.

The cost of the convention and the hotel prior to WAFC waiving the students' fee is $2,840.
After WAFC waives the registration fee, the remaining amount owed for hotel accommodations is
$1,350 The registration fee for the conference is $1,490. Your husband's employer, Raley's, is
paying for the hotel accommodations. You will neither attend the convention nor use the hotel
accommodations.

To the best of your knowledge, you have not been involved in making decisions or
providing advice involving the WAFC. As far as you know, the WAFC does not know who you are
nor do they know that you are a public official. The benefit provided to your husband was not
influenced by your position as a public official. The WAFC waives the registration fee of all
students that attend the FIM program.

ANALYSIS

Section 82028(a) defines a "gift" as any payment that confers a personal benefit on the
recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received. As a general rule,
when a gift is received by the spouse of a public official and the official enjoys a direct benefit from
the gift, the official is required to disclose the gift on his or her statement of economic interests.

To reduce improper influence on public officials, one of the ways the Act regulates the
receipt of gifts by public officials regards gifts to an official through a family member:

Regulation 18943(c) provides that gifts to the benefit of your spouse will be considered gifts
to you if either of the following applies:

"(1) There is no established working, social, or similar relationship between the donor and
the family member that would suggest an association between the donor and the family member
suitable or appropriate for making the type of payment provided to the family member."

"(2) There is evidence to suggest the donor had a purpose to influence the official."

File No. A-19-033
Page No. 3

Your husband has a well-established working relationship with Raley's Supermarkets. Specifically, he has an almost five-year working relationship with the food retailer and was selected by Raley's from a pool of other management level employee applicants to attend the management training program. As such, he has established "independent relationship" that lead to him receiving the offer to participate in the FIM program. Your husband's FIM class was invited to speak at and/or attend WAFC's annual convention. As such, WAFC's waiver off its registration fee solely arises from your husband's independent relationship with Raley's and his participation in the FIM program. Accordingly, the Act's gift prohibitions are inapplicable to the indirect benefits you may receive from the waiver.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Ryan O'Connor
Counsel, Legal Division

RPOC:jgl

As a public official subject to the Act's gift provisions, is Western Association of Food 
Chains' ("WAFC") waiver of your husband's registration fee considered a gift to you?

sections.analysis:
Section 82028(a) defines a "gift" as any payment that confers a personal benefit on the 
recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received. As a general rule, 
when a gift is received by the spouse of a public official and the official enjoys a direct benefit from 
the gift, the official is required to disclose the gift on his or her statement of economic interests. 
To requce improper influence on public officials, one of the ways the Act regulates the 
receipt of gifts by public officials regards gifts to an official through a family member: 
Regulation 18943(c) provides that gifts to the benefit of your spouse will be considered gifts 
to you if either of the following applies:

the family member that would suggest an association between the donor and the family member 
suitable or appropriate for making the type of payment provided to the family member." 
"(2) There is evidence to suggest the donor had a purpose to influence the official."  
Your husband has a well-established working relationship with Raley's Supermarkets. 
Specifically, he has an almost five-year working relationship with the food retailer and was selected 
by Raley's from a pool of other management level employee applicants to attend the management 
training program. As such; he has an established "independent relationship" that lead to him 
receiving the offer to participate in the FIM program. Your husband's FIM class was invited to 
speak at and/or attend WAFC's annual convention. As such, WAFC's waiver off its registration fee 
solely arises from your husband's independent relationship with Raley's and his participation in the 
FIM program. Accordingly, the Act's gift prohibitions are inapplicable to the indirect benefits you
